tool.name: GaCDI Manifest Builder (GDC)
tool.description: build a filtered GDC download manifest and enriched metadata table
tool.command: gacdi-manifest gdc #if str($core.project).strip() != '' --project '$core.project' #end if #if str($core.data_category) != '' --data-category '$core.data_category' #end if #if str($core.data_type).strip() != '' --data-type '$core.data_type' #end if #if str($core.access) != '' --access '$core.access' #end if #if $core.file_id_list --file-id-list '$core.file_id_list' #end if #if $core.case_list --case-list '$core.case_list' #end if #if $core.sample_list --sample-list '$core.sample_list' #end if #if str($adv.primary_site).strip() != '' --primary-site '$adv.primary_site' #end if #if str($adv.disease_type).strip() != '' --disease-type '$adv.disease_type' #end if #if str($adv.experimental_strategy) != '' --experimental-strategy '$adv.experimental_strategy' #end if #if str($adv.workflow_type).strip() != '' --workflow-type '$adv.workflow_type' #end if #if str($adv.platform).strip() != '' --platform '$adv.platform' #end if #if str($adv.data_format) != '' --data-format '$adv.data_format' #end if #if str($adv.sample_type).strip() != '' --sample-type '$adv.sample_type' #end if #for $ef in $gdc_filter.extra_filters --extra-filter 'field=${ef.field};op=${ef.op};values=${ef.values}' #end for #if $gdc_filter.raw_filters --raw-filters '$gdc_filter.raw_filters' #end if #if $count_only --count-only #else #if str($adv.max_files) != '' --max-files $adv.max_files #end if #end if #if $adv.enrich.enable == 'yes' #if $adv.enrich.cbio.use == 'yes' --cbioportal-study '$adv.enrich.cbio.study' #if str($adv.enrich.cbio.attrs).strip() != '' --cbioportal-attrs '$adv.enrich.cbio.attrs' #end if #end if #if $adv.enrich.table.use == 'yes' --annotation-tsv '$adv.enrich.table.tsv' --annotation-key-col '$adv.enrich.table.key_col' #end if --join-level '$adv.enrich.join_level' #if not $adv.enrich.trim_vial --no-trim-vial #end if #end if #for $mf in $adv.metadata_filters --metadata-filter 'column=${mf.column};op=${mf.op};values=${mf.values}' #end for --manifest-out '$manifest' --metadata-out '$metadata' --report-out '$report' --selection-manifest-out '$selection_manifest' --selection-metadata-out '$selection_metadata' --selection-provenance-out '$selection_provenance'

param.name: project
param.label: Project
param.help: GDC project to pull from, e.g. TCGA-BRCA. Only files from this project are included. Comma-separate for several.

param.name: data_category
param.label: Data category
param.help: Broad kind of data. Narrows the manifest to this category.

param.name: data_type
param.label: Data type
param.help: The specific files to download, e.g. Slide Image or Gene Expression Quantification. Usually your main choice.

param.name: access
param.label: Access
param.help: Open files download freely; controlled files need a token at download time.

param.name: file_id_list
param.label: GDC file IDs
param.help: Optional allowlist with one GDC file UUID per line. This is combined with the guided filters.

param.name: case_list
param.label: Case submitter barcodes
param.help: Optional allowlist with one GDC case submitter barcode per line (for example, a TCGA participant barcode). Native case UUIDs are not accepted by this field.

param.name: sample_list
param.label: Sample submitter barcodes
param.help: Optional allowlist with one GDC sample submitter barcode per line. Native sample UUIDs are not accepted by this field. This is the preferred sample-level selection input.

section.name: gdc_filter
section.title: Filter by GDC field (part of the GDC query)

repeat.name: extra_filters
repeat.title: Custom GDC field filter
repeat.help: Narrow the GDC query on ANY GDC field, not just the ones above. This is applied by GDC when the query runs, so it decides which files are found (and therefore what ends up in both the manifest and the metadata) — it does not filter your output files after the fact. To filter the final table on a column this tool generated (e.g. keep only samples with a subtype), use 'Filter the final table by metadata column' under Advanced options instead.

param.name: field
param.label: GDC field name
param.help: Use the GDC's own field name in dot-notation, e.g. cases.samples.sample_type, cases.demographic.gender, cases.diagnoses.primary_diagnosis, analysis.workflow_type. File-level fields have no prefix (e.g. experimental_strategy, platform, access); case/sample/clinical fields start with 'cases.'. Browse the full list at https://docs.gdc.cancer.gov/API/Users_Guide/Appendix_A_Available_Fields/ or fetch it live from https://api.gdc.cancer.gov/files/_mapping . The names here are GDC field names, NOT the column headers in the metadata output.

param.name: op
param.label: Match or exclude
param.help: Keep files whose field is one of your values, or exclude them.

param.name: values
param.label: Value(s)
param.help: Values to match, comma-separated. Must match GDC's stored values exactly, e.g. 'Primary Tumor' or 'female'.

param.name: raw_filters
param.label: Raw GDC filters JSON
param.help: Power users: a full GDC 'filters' object, combined (AND) with everything above. Use this for operators the simple form can't express (ranges, missing-value checks, nested OR).

param.name: count_only
param.label: Preview counts only (don't build the manifest yet)
param.help: Reports how many files match, with a breakdown, so you can refine filters before building the full manifest.

param.name: primary_site
param.label: Primary site
param.help: Keep only cases from this anatomic site, e.g. Breast or Lung. Comma-separate for several.

param.name: disease_type
param.label: Disease type
param.help: Keep only this disease classification, e.g. Ductal and Lobular Neoplasms.

param.name: experimental_strategy
param.label: Experimental strategy
param.help: Keep only files produced by this assay.

param.name: workflow_type
param.label: Analysis workflow type
param.help: Keep only files from this analysis pipeline, e.g. 'STAR - Counts'. Picks one processing variant.

param.name: platform
param.label: Platform
param.help: Keep only this sequencing/array platform, e.g. Illumina.

param.name: data_format
param.label: Data format
param.help: Keep only this file format.

param.name: sample_type
param.label: Sample type
param.help: Keep only this sample kind, e.g. Primary Tumor or Solid Tissue Normal.

repeat.name: metadata_filters
repeat.title: Filter the final table by metadata column
repeat.help: A second filtering layer applied AFTER the query and annotation join, on the metadata columns this tool generated (the headers you see in the metadata output — e.g. sample_type, vital_status, or an enrichment column like SUBTYPE or ER_STATUS_BY_IHC). Use it to clean the final manifest down to the samples you want: keep only rows where a column is present (non-blank), or matches specific value(s). A file stays in the manifest if at least one of its samples passes. Tip: run once to see the metadata columns, then add filters referring to them by their exact header name.

param.name: column
param.label: Metadata column
param.help: Exact column header from the metadata output, e.g. ER_STATUS_BY_IHC, SUBTYPE, sample_type, vital_status.

param.name: op
param.label: Condition
param.help: How to test each row's value in that column.

param.name: values
param.label: Value(s)
param.help: Comma-separated. Required for 'is one of', 'is not', and 'contains'; leave blank for 'is present' / 'is blank'.

param.name: enable
param.label: Add clinical/annotation columns to the metadata?
param.help: Adds per-sample columns (subtype, receptor status, your own labels). Does not change which files are downloaded.

param.name: use
param.label: Add cBioPortal subtype/clinical columns?
param.help: Pulls curated per-sample attributes (e.g. PAM50 subtype, ER/PR/HER2 status) from one cBioPortal study.

param.name: study
param.label: cBioPortal study id(s) (required)
param.help: One id, or comma-separate several to COMBINE them, e.g. brca_tcga_pan_can_atlas_2018,brca_tcga (PAM50 subtype + ER/PR/HER2 IHC). Ids only — no flag, no spaces inside an id. Find each in its cBioPortal URL after 'id='.

validator.message: Enter study id(s) only, e.g. brca_tcga or a,b,c — no spaces inside an id, no flag names.

param.name: attrs
param.label: Which attributes to add
param.help: Comma-separated ids, e.g. SUBTYPE,ER_STATUS_BY_IHC,PR_STATUS_BY_IHC. Leave blank (or type 'all') for every attribute.

param.name: use
param.label: Merge your own annotation table?
param.help: Adds columns from a TSV you upload (e.g. histology labels not in any API), matched by barcode.

param.name: tsv
param.label: Annotation table (TSV, required)
param.help: A per-sample table; one of its columns holds the barcode used for matching.

param.name: key_col
param.label: Barcode column in your table (required)
param.help: Name of the column holding the sample/patient barcode.

param.name: join_level
param.label: Annotation join level
param.help: How precisely annotations are matched to each file.

param.name: trim_vial
param.label: Treat 01A and 01 as the same sample
param.help: Ignore the vial letter when matching annotations (recommended).

param.name: max_files
param.label: Maximum number of files
param.help: Stop after this many files — handy for a quick test. Leave blank for all matches.

tool.help:
**GaCDI Manifest Builder (GDC)**
Builds a **targeted selection bundle** from the NCI `Genomic Data Commons <https://gdc.cancer.gov/>`_ by filtering, so you fetch exactly the files you want instead of a whole dataset. Guided filters can be combined with uploaded file, case, or sample ID allowlists. It produces compatibility outputs plus the canonical three-file bundle used by the downloader:
1. **GDC manifest** — strict ``id / filename / md5 / size / state`` format, ready to feed straight into the **GaCDI GDC** importer or ``gdc-client``. 2. **metadata** — a research table joining the file records to sample barcodes and any clinical/molecular annotations you enable (GDC fields, cBioPortal subtypes such as PAM50 and ER/PR/HER2, and/or an uploaded annotation table). It also carries workflow-relevant columns — ``data_format``, ``data_type``, ``experimental_strategy``, ``workflow_type``, ``platform``, ``primary_site``, ``disease_type`` — and a ``galaxy_ext`` column giving the best-effort Galaxy datatype for each file, so downstream tools know how to interpret it. 3. **report** — match/precision QC: counts, unmatched files, unused annotations and colliding keys. In *Preview counts only* mode this holds the match count and a facet breakdown so you can refine filters cheaply before building. 4. **canonical selection manifest** — one validated row per remote retrieval asset. 5. **canonical selection metadata** — case/sample associations for every selected asset. 6. **selection provenance** — the normalized query, counts, warnings, and SHA-256 hashes that bind the canonical manifest and metadata into one reproducible bundle.
Connect the three canonical outputs to **GaCDI GDC → Canonical selection bundle**. The original GDC manifest output remains available for existing saved workflows.
**Getting started**
Fill in **What to download** (usually a *Project* plus a *Data type*) and run with *Preview counts only* first to see how many files match. When the count looks right, turn the preview off to build the manifest. The guided facets, enrichment, and both filter layers below are all optional.
**Two kinds of filter (they work at different stages)**
1. **Filter by GDC field** *(part of the GDC query)* — narrows the query on any GDC field, so it decides which files GDC returns. Use GDC's own field names in dot-notation (e.g. ``cases.samples.sample_type``, ``cases.demographic.gender``); the complete list is in the `GDC Available Fields <https://docs.gdc.cancer.gov/API/Users_Guide/Appendix_A_Available_Fields/>`_ page, or fetch it live from ``https://api.gdc.cancer.gov/files/_mapping``. These are the API's field names, **not** the column headers in the metadata output.
2. **Filter the final table by metadata column** *(Advanced options)* — a second layer applied **after** the query and annotation join, on the columns this tool generated (the headers in the metadata output — including any clinical/enrichment columns you added). Use it to trim the final manifest to just the samples you want: e.g. keep only rows where ``ER_STATUS_BY_IHC`` *is present*, or where ``sample_type`` *is one of* ``Primary Tumor``. A file stays in the manifest as long as at least one of its samples passes. A good workflow is to build once, look at the metadata columns, then re-run adding metadata-column filters that reference those exact headers.
**Why two files?** ``gdc-client`` rejects a manifest that contains extra clinical columns or lacks ``state`` — so the lean manifest is kept separate from the rich metadata table, which you join to the downloaded files afterwards by barcode.
